title: missing_variable_table
summary: Explicit variable-sweep or optimization tables disappear from the canonical S2-2 evidence handoff despite formulation-bearing local structure.

title: table_flattening
summary: Flattened or placeholder-heavy tables leak noisy rows and weak role structure into S2-2 evidence and prompt-facing table payloads.

title: false_role_coverage
summary: S2-2 design status can look covered even when role selection misses the execution-critical VARIABLE_TABLE or optimization table needed for downstream capability.

title: evidence_budget_overflow
summary: Prompt evidence exceeds the healthy size policy even after selector success, increasing prefill and no-first-token risk at S2-4b.

title: near_duplicate_evidence
summary: Near-duplicate narrative or table payloads are emitted into canonical evidence blocks, wasting budget and inflating prompt size.

title: Govern stage2_variable_sweep_and_table_compaction_v1 as the maintained S2-2 class-level repair
summary: Treat variable-sweep rescue, evidence-table compaction, duplicate suppression, and S2-2/S2-3 boundary validation as the governed maintained S2-2 repair set.

title: Decision: Lock the summary-only coverage-first S2-2 / S2-4a selector contract
summary: Keep all S2-4a table evidence summary-only, reduce selector authority to denoising/coverage/packing only, and return semantic table scoping to the LLM.

title: S2-2b confirmed-noise-only table preservation
summary: Irreversible S2-2b table removal is limited to confirmed pure noise; if a table is not confirmed noise, preserve it in the pre-LLM authority surface.

title: S2-3/S2-4a summary neutrality and structure-first summary contract
summary: The maintained S2-3/S2-4a summary path is neutral across preserved tables; the main residual risk is lossy compression, and header schema plus first-column row identity are the primary summary contract while sample rows are optional aids only.

title: Authority reopen metadata is deterministic execution-side metadata
summary: authority_run_dir, authority_payload_root, and related reopen handles are deterministic execution-side metadata and must not be treated as LLM semantic content or depend on the LLM to transmit them.

title: Prefer replay from frozen raw responses when the LLM-facing contract is unchanged
summary: When LLM task, prompt content, evidence content, model, and generation settings are unchanged, downstream deterministic contract changes should prefer replay from frozen raw responses over fresh live calls if execution-side metadata can be lawfully reattached.

title: 5ZXYABSU selector/preservation family
summary: Formulation-bearing Table 1 and Table 2 survive S2-2a but disappear before replay reopen because only Table 14 remains preserved downstream.

title: 5GIF3D8W non-DOE single-variable recovery family
summary: A paper has an explicit baseline or optimized formulation table plus later single-variable exploration groups, but those formulation instances are not recoverable by DOE logic or Cartesian expansion.

title: Bounded simple formulation-table deterministic enumeration after semantic authorization
summary: Stage2 may deterministically enumerate base rows for low-ambiguity non-DOE full_formulation tables after LLM table authorization using preserved normalized_table_payload authority; this path does not require LLM row-level output and does not apply to DOE, sweep recovery, or cross-table decode cases.

title: simple_formulation_table_family_collapse
summary: A low-ambiguity formulation-bearing table is preserved and semantically authorized, but only a family-level semantic object survives and no deterministic base-row enumeration occurs.

title: Decision: Restore UFXX9WXE DOE target binding with file-derived table IDs and tolerant numbered-row detection
summary: Bounded replay validation restored UFXX9WXE deterministic DOE enumeration to 26 rows by preferring file-derived __table_N__ identity over conflicting caption-derived numbers and by tolerating spaced numbered labels like 7 . during numbered-row detection.

title: UFXX9WXE DOE authorized_target_unresolved with preserved Table10 asset mis-identified
summary: LLM declares DOE scope for Table 10, but DOE function unit stays authorized=yes called=no and numbered DOE rows are absent from the completed Stage2 candidate surface.

title: Decision: Recover corrupted split-column variable-sweep tables with source-backed row recovery
summary: Use bounded source-backed row recovery in table_row_expansion_v1 for LLM-authorized variable-sweep tables whose preserved payload stays repair_insufficient/unrepaired_corrupted.

title: Decision: Collapse later measurement-only rows that reuse DOE labels onto existing DOE formulation cores
summary: For DOE papers, later non-DOE measurement/post-processing rows that reuse a unique deterministic DOE formulation label and only add before/after processing characterization variables must collapse onto the existing DOE formulation core rather than create a new formulation.

title: Decision: Recover anchorless sequential-optimization single-variable rows from stagewise source-text contracts when row-level raw replay collapsed to family summaries
summary: When a replayed sequential-optimization paper preserves stagewise optimization semantics and selected-condition hints but no longer materializes row-level candidates, Stage2 may recover synthesis-defining single-variable rows directly from explicit stagewise source-text level lists, provided downstream/post-processing variable axes are excluded and an explicit anchor scope is not already available elsewhere in the document.

title: mismatch
summary: The current repo state still has important unresolved problems across architecture, benchmarking, identity binding, audit usability, and modeling readiness. The most serious issue is an architecture/runtime mismatch: the governed architecture now freezes Stage2 authority to an LLM semantic-discovery boundary, but the current machine-readable active run and parts of the human-facing docs still resolve to a deterministic semantic-emitter lineage. The active DEV15 semantic run also shows significant row-identity drift once identity-bearing variables are preserved, and the benchmark comparison workflow is not fully governed because the maintained compare entrypoint could not consume the current GT workbook shape and a read-only custom evaluator was used instead. Downstream, Layer2 binding remains only partially generalized, Stage5 closure rules remain intentionally narrow, Layer3 is still fragmented, evidence binding is still coarse in key places, and normalization / chemical-entity modeling surfaces remain incomplete.
